news.news: China's National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) approved Exdensur (depemokimab), an ultra-long-acting monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (CRSwNP) in adults. The approval is based on data from the ANCHOR-1 and ANCHOR-2 phase III trials showing significant improvements in nasal polyp size and obstruction.

Gilead Sciences entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Tubulis GmbH for $3.15 billion in upfront cash, plus up to $1.85 billion in milestone payments. The acquisition adds Tubulis' next-generation antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) platform and clinical assets, including TUB-040, to Gilead's oncology pipeline.

CRISPR Therapeutics announced that its senior management team will present at the 25th Annual Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference on April 13, 2026. The company highlighted its leadership in gene editing, including the approved CASGEVY therapy and its proprietary SyNTase editing platform.

Chugai Pharmaceutical and Zenyaku Kogyo announced the termination of their co-promotion agreement for Rituxan (rituximab), an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody, in Japan. Effective September 3, 2026, Zenyaku Kogyo will assume sole responsibility for sales and medical information for the product.
